metadata.description: schwannden's personal collection of shareable agent skills.

plugins.name: personas
plugins.description: Writing and coaching personas: technical-writing voice, reflective/spiritual essays, and a leadership coach.

plugins.name: fastapi-backend
plugins.description: Python/FastAPI backend craft: response envelopes, async tests, Docker, and Django REST Framework.

plugins.name: ai-engineering
plugins.description: AI/LLM engineering: thin agent loops on the Anthropic SDK, disciplined local-model benchmarking, and local media transcription.

plugins.name: operations
plugins.description: Service operations: tool-agnostic health monitoring and end-to-end incident triage, both self-evolving templates.

plugins.name: dev-workflow
plugins.description: Engineering workflow: PR walkthroughs, batch dependency-upgrade risk scans, and Jira/Confluence REST recipes.

plugins.name: authoring
plugins.description: Meta-skills for authoring: test-driven skill writing and high-quality AGENTS.md files.

plugins.name: engineering
plugins.description: Portable engineering process across the build lifecycle: a vendor-neutral system-design partner (with a mock-interview mode and a 15-design reference library), a multi-phase feature-development loop, and a multi-dimension code-review methodology.

plugins.name: web-quality
plugins.description: Audit a live website across five dimensions — mobile-friendliness, accessibility (WCAG 2.2 AA), PWA-installability, SEO/Core Web Vitals, and GEO/AEO (getting crawled, rendered, and cited by AI answer engines) — with curl-based static passes plus per-dimension checklists and the lab/manual tools the static passes can't replace.

plugins.name: feature-dev
plugins.description: Guided, gated feature development: a /feature-dev command that explores the codebase, asks clarifying questions, designs candidate architectures, implements, then auto-reviews — backed by code-explorer and code-architect subagents.

plugins.name: pr-review
plugins.description: Comprehensive multi-agent PR review: a /review-pr command that inspects the diff, selects applicable specialist subagents (code quality, tests, docs, error handling, type design, simplification), runs them in parallel, and aggregates findings by severity.
